### `auth/main.py`

```python
"""
Login endpoint.

Transport note: TLS termination is handled at the reverse proxy (nginx/ALB).
This service MUST NOT be exposed directly on a plaintext port in production.
HSTS is set here as defense-in-depth; the proxy must enforce TLS.
Rate limiting is implemented here; a gateway-level layer is additive, not a
substitute (gateway bypass = no rate limiting without this layer).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import hmac
import time
import uuid
from typing import Annotated

import structlog
from argon2 import PasswordHasher
from argon2.exceptions import VerifyMismatchError, VerificationError, InvalidHashError
from fastapi import Cookie, FastAPI, Request, Response, status
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

from auth.config import settings
from auth.logging_config import configure_logging
from auth.models import LoginRequest, LoginResponse
from auth.rate_limiter import rate_limiter
from auth.security_headers import SecurityHeadersMiddleware
from auth.session_store import session_store

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Bootstrap
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
configure_logging(level="DEBUG" if not settings.IS_PROD else "INFO")
log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

app = FastAPI(
    # Disable automatic debug info in responses
    debug=False,
    # Suppress FastAPI's default "Server" header contribution
    openapi_url=None if settings.IS_PROD else "/openapi.json",
)
app.add_middleware(SecurityHeadersMiddleware)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Password hasher — module-level singleton
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_ph = PasswordHasher(
    time_cost=settings.ARGON2_TIME_COST,
    memory_cost=settings.ARGON2_MEMORY_COST,
    parallelism=settings.ARGON2_PARALLELISM,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake user store — replace with your DB layer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Passwords are stored as argon2id hashes.
# To generate: _ph.hash(_pepper(raw_password))
# The _DUMMY_HASH is used for constant-time comparison when the user doesn't
# exist, preventing a timing oracle on user enumeration.
def _pepper(password: str) -> str:
    """
    Mix in the server-side pepper via HMAC before hashing.
    Protects against DB-only exfiltration (the hash is useless without the pepper).
    """
    if not settings.PEPPER:
        return password
    return hmac.new(settings.PEPPER, password.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()


# Pre-computed dummy hash for timing-safe "user not found" path.
# Recompute if you change argon2 parameters.
_DUMMY_HASH: str = _ph.hash(_pepper("__dummy_password_never_matches__"))

# Stub user DB — replace with actual repository
_USER_DB: dict[str, dict] = {
    # username -> {id, password_hash}
    # In production: load from DB, never store plaintext
}


def _get_user(username: str) -> dict | None:
    """Repository stub. Replace with an async DB call."""
    return _USER_DB.get(username)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _no_cache_response(data: dict, status_code: int) -> JSONResponse:
    """All auth responses must not be cached."""
    resp = JSONResponse(content=data, status_code=status_code)
    resp.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    resp.headers["Pragma"] = "no-cache"
    return resp


def _set_session_cookie(response: Response, session_id: str) -> None:
    """
    Set the session cookie with all required security attributes.
    __Host- prefix enforces: Secure + no Domain attribute + Path=/.
    """
    response.set_cookie(
        key=settings.COOKIE_NAME,
        value=session_id,
        httponly=True,
        secure=True,
        samesite="lax",
        max_age=settings.SESSION_ABSOLUTE_SECONDS,
        path="/",
        # Do NOT set domain= — __Host- prefix requires its absence
    )


def _invalid_credentials_response() -> JSONResponse:
    """
    Single, identical response for all authentication failures.
    Same message, same status code — no enumeration oracle (CWE-204).
    """
    return _no_cache_response(
        {"error": "invalid_credentials", "message": "Authentication failed."},
        status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
    )


def _rate_limited_response(retry_after: int) -> JSONResponse:
    resp = _no_cache_response(
        {
            "error": "rate_limited",
            "message": "Too many requests. Please retry later.",
            "retry_after": retry_after,
        },
        status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
    )
    resp.headers["Retry-After"] = str(retry_after)
    # IETF draft-ietf-httpapi-ratelimit-headers
    resp.headers["RateLimit-Policy"] = (
        f'"ip";q={settings.IP_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS};'
        f'w={settings.IP_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS}'
    )
    return resp


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Login endpoint
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.post(
    "/auth/login",
    response_model=LoginResponse,
    status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK,
    # Explicit response codes for OpenAPI — no accidental info leak
    responses={
        200: {"description": "Authenticated; session cookie set."},
        401: {"description": "Authentication failed."},
        429: {"description": "Rate limited."},
    },
)
async def login(
    request: Request,
    body: LoginRequest,
) -> Response:
    """
    Authenticate a user and issue a session cookie.

    Security properties:
    - argon2id password verification with pepper
    - Constant-time comparison; dummy hash run when user doesn't exist
    - Identical error response for "no user" and "wrong password"
    - Per-IP and per-account sliding-window rate limiting
    - Per-account exponential-backoff lockout after repeated failures
    - Session ID issued from CSPRNG, stored server-side, set as HttpOnly cookie
    - Rehash-on-login when argon2 parameters are below current policy
    """
    request_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    # Bind to structlog context — all subsequent log calls in this request
    # automatically include these fields (no manual passing).
    structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars()
    structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(
        request_id=request_id,
        path=request.url.path,
        method=request.method,
    )

    client_ip: str = (
        request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For", "").split(",")[0].strip()
        or request.client.host
        or "unknown"
    )
    # NOTE: In production, trust X-Forwarded-For only if your proxy sets it
    # reliably and strips attacker-supplied values. Otherwise use request.client.host.

    username = body.username  # already stripped/lowercased by validator

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 1. IP-level rate limit (unauthenticated backstop)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    ip_allowed, ip_retry_after = rate_limiter.check_ip(client_ip)
    if not ip_allowed:
        log.warning(
            "login_rate_limited_ip",
            client_ip=client_ip,
            retry_after=ip_retry_after,
        )
        return _rate_limited_response(ip_retry_after)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 2. Account-level rate limit
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    acct_allowed, acct_retry_after = rate_limiter.check_account(username)
    if not acct_allowed:
        log.warning(
            "login_rate_limited_account",
            # Do NOT log the username in a way that reveals existence;
            # log a hash so we can correlate without exposing the value.
            username_hash=hashlib.sha256(username.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            retry_after=acct_retry_after,
        )
        return _rate_limited_response(acct_retry_after)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 3. Account lockout (exponential backoff)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    lockout_delay = rate_limiter.get_lockout_delay(username)
    if lockout_delay > 0:
        log.warning(
            "login_account_locked",
            username_hash=hashlib.sha256(username.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            lockout_seconds=lockout_delay,
        )
        return _rate_limited_response(lockout_delay)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 4. Credential verification
    #    ALWAYS run the hash even when the user doesn't exist — timing oracle
    #    prevention. The dummy hash ensures argon2 work is done regardless.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    user = _get_user(username)
    stored_hash = user["password_hash"] if user else _DUMMY_HASH
    peppered_attempt = _pepper(body.password)

    verified = False
    needs_rehash = False
    try:
        _ph.verify(stored_hash, peppered_attempt)
        verified = True
        needs_rehash = _ph.check_needs_rehash(stored_hash)
    except VerifyMismatchError:
        # Wrong password — fall through to unified failure response
        pass
    except (VerificationError, InvalidHashError) as exc:
        # Hash is malformed — this is an internal error, not a user error.
        # Log it (without the hash value) but return the same 401 to the client.
        log.error(
            "login_hash_verification_error",
            error_type=type(exc).__name__,
            # Never log the hash or the password
        )

    # User doesn't exist OR password wrong → identical response
    if not verified or user is None:
        rate_limiter.record_failure(username)
        log.info(
            "login_failed",
            client_ip=client_ip,
            # username intentionally omitted from INFO log — use hash for correlation
            username_hash=hashlib.sha256(username.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            outcome="invalid_credentials",
        )
        return _invalid_credentials_response()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 5. Rehash if parameters are below current policy
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    if needs_rehash:
        new_hash = _ph.hash(peppered_attempt)
        # Update the stored hash — replace with your DB update call
        _USER_DB[username]["password_hash"] = new_hash
        log.info("login_password_rehashed", user_id=user["id"])

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # 6. Issue session — new ID on every login (CWE-384 prevention)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    session_id = session_store.create(user["id"])

    log.info(
        "login_success",
        user_id=user["id"],
        client_ip=client_ip,
        # Never log session_id — it's a bearer credential
    )

    response = _no_cache_response(
        {"message": "Authenticated successfully."},
        status.HTTP_200_OK,
    )
    _set_session_cookie(response, session_id)

    # Return the request_id so clients can correlate support requests
    response.headers["X-Request-Id"] = request_id

    return response


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logout endpoint (demonstrates server-side session invalidation)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.post("/auth/logout", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def logout(
    request: Request,
    session_cookie: Annotated[str | None, Cookie(alias=settings.COOKIE_NAME)] = None,
) -> Response:
    """
    Invalidate the session server-side and clear the cookie.
    Clearing the cookie client-side alone is insufficient (CWE-613).
    """
    if session_cookie:
        session_store.delete(session_cookie)
        log.info("logout", outcome="session_invalidated")
    else:
        log.info("logout", outcome="no_session")

    response = Response(status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
    # Delete the cookie
    response.delete_cookie(
        key=settings.COOKIE_NAME,
        path="/",
        secure=True,
        httponly=True,
        samesite="lax",
    )
    # Tell the browser to clear cookies and storage (defense in depth)
    response.headers["Clear-Site-Data"] = '"cookies", "storage"'
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    return response


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Global exception handler — never leak internals to the client (CWE-209)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.exception_handler(Exception)
async def unhandled_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception):
    cid = structlog.contextvars.get_contextvars().get("request_id", str(uuid.uuid4()))
    log.exception(
        "unhandled_exception",
        error_type=type(exc).__name__,
        # Stack trace captured by structlog's ExceptionRenderer into one field
    )
    return _no_cache_response(
        {"error": "internal_error", "cid": cid},
        status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
    )
```

```python
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestLoginSuccess:

        assert resp.status_code == 200, resp.text

    def test_response_body_has_message(self, client, registered_user):

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Failed login — wrong password
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestWrongPassword:
    def test_returns_401(self, client, registered_user):
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], "wrong-password")
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_generic_error_message(self, client, registered_user):

        assert resp.json()["error"] == "invalid_credentials"

    def test_no_session_cookie(self, client, registered_user):

        assert settings.COOKIE_NAME not in resp.cookies


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Failed login — no such user
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestNoSuchUser:
    def test_returns_401(self, client):
        resp = login(client, "ghost@example.com", "any-password")
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_identical_error_message_to_wrong_password(self, client, registered_user):
        """
        CRITICAL: same error body for 'no user' and 'wrong password'.
        Any difference is an enumeration oracle (CWE-204).
        """
        resp_no_user = login(client, "ghost@example.com", "any-password")
        resp_wrong_pw = login(client, registered_user["username"], "wrong-password")

        assert resp_no_user.status_code == resp_wrong_pw.status_code
        assert resp_no_user.json() == resp_wrong_pw.json()

    def test_no_session_cookie(self, client):
        resp = login(client, "ghost@example.com", "any-password")
        assert settings.COOKIE_NAME not in resp.cookies


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Timing oracle — no-such-user vs wrong-password
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestTimingOracle:
    """
    Verify that the dummy-hash path runs argon2 work regardless of user existence.
    We can't guarantee exact timing equality in a test environment, but we can
    assert both paths take > 50ms (argon2 is running) and their ratio is < 3x.
    A ratio > 3x would indicate one path is skipping the hash entirely.
    """

    SAMPLES = 3

    def _measure(self, client, username: str, password: str) -> float:
        times = []
        for _ in range(self.SAMPLES):
            start = time.perf_counter()
            login(client, username, password)
            times.append(time.perf_counter() - start)
        return sum(times) / len(times)

    def test_both_paths_run_argon2(self, client, registered_user):
        t_no_user = self._measure(client, "ghost@example.com", "any-password")
        t_wrong_pw = self._measure(client, registered_user["username"], "wrong-password")

        # Both must take > 50ms — argon2 must be running on both paths
        assert t_no_user > 0.05, (
            f"No-user path too fast ({t_no_user:.3f}s) — dummy hash may not be running"
        )
        assert t_wrong_pw > 0.05, (
            f"Wrong-pw path too fast ({t_wrong_pw:.3f}s) — argon2 may not be running"
        )

        # Ratio must be < 3x (generous for CI variance)
        ratio = max(t_no_user, t_wrong_pw) / min(t_no_user, t_wrong_pw)
        assert ratio < 3.0, (
            f"Timing ratio {ratio:.2f}x too large — potential enumeration oracle. "
            f"no_user={t_no_user:.3f}s, wrong_pw={t_wrong_pw:.3f}s"
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Rate limiting
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestRateLimiting:
    def test_ip_rate_limit_triggers_429(self, client):
        """Exceed the IP limit; must get 429 with Retry-After."""
        # Patch the limit to something small for the test
        import auth.main as main_module
        main_module.rate_limiter._ip_counters.clear()

        with patch.object(settings, "IP_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS", 3):
            fresh_rl = RateLimiter()
            main_module.rate_limiter = fresh_rl

            responses = [
                login(client, f"user{i}@example.com", "pw") for i in range(5)
            ]

        status_codes = [r.status_code for r in responses]
        assert 429 in status_codes, f"Expected 429 in {status_codes}"

    def test_429_has_retry_after_header(self, client):
        import auth.main as main_module
        with patch.object(settings, "IP_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS", 2):
            fresh_rl = RateLimiter()
            main_module.rate_limiter = fresh_rl

            for i in range(4):
                resp = login(client, f"u{i}@example.com", "pw")
                if resp.status_code == 429:
                    assert "retry-after" in resp.headers, "429 must include Retry-After"
                    assert int(resp.headers["retry-after"]) > 0
                    return
        pytest.fail("429 was never returned")

    def test_account_lockout_after_threshold(self, client, registered_user):
        """Repeated failures for the same account trigger lockout."""
        import auth.main as main_module
        with patch.object(settings, "ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD", 3):
            fresh_rl = RateLimiter()
            main_module.rate_limiter = fresh_rl

            responses = []
            for _ in range(6):
                responses.append(
                    login(client, registered_user["username"], "wrong-pw")
                )

        assert any(r.status_code == 429 for r in responses), (
            "Expected 429 after lockout threshold"
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Logout
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestLogout:
    def test_logout_invalidates_session_server_side(self, client, registered_user):
        import auth.main as main_module

        # Login
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        session_id = resp.cookies.get(settings.COOKIE_NAME)
        assert session_id

        # Verify session exists
        assert main_module.session_store.get(session_id) is not None

        # Logout
        logout_resp = client.post(
            "/auth/logout",
            cookies={settings.COOKIE_NAME: session_id},
        )
        assert logout_resp.status_code == 204

        # Session must be gone server-side
        assert main_module.session_store.get(session_id) is None, (
            "Session must be invalidated server-side on logout"
        )

    def test_logout_sets_clear_site_data_header(self, client, registered_user):
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        session_id = resp.cookies.get(settings.COOKIE_NAME)

        logout_resp = client.post(
            "/auth/logout",
            cookies={settings.COOKIE_NAME: session_id},
        )
        assert "clear-site-data" in logout_resp.headers


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Security headers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSecurityHeaders:
    REQUIRED_HEADERS = [
        "strict-transport-security",
        "x-content-type-options",
        "referrer-policy",
        "content-security-policy",
        "cross-origin-opener-policy",
    ]

    def test_security_headers_on_success(self, client, registered_user):
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        for header in self.REQUIRED_HEADERS:
            assert header in resp.headers, f"Missing security header: {header}"

    def test_security_headers_on_failure(self, client):
        resp = login(client, "nobody", "nothing")
        for header in self.REQUIRED_HEADERS:
            assert header in resp.headers, f"Missing security header on 401: {header}"

    def test_hsts_includes_subdomains(self, client, registered_user):
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        hsts = resp.headers.get("strict-transport-security", "")
        assert "includesubdomains" in hsts.lower()

    def test_nosniff(self, client, registered_user):
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        assert resp.headers.get("x-content-type-options") == "nosniff"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. No secrets in logs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestNoSecretsInLogs:
    """
    Verify the redaction filter catches passwords, tokens, and JWTs.
    We capture structlog output and assert no sensitive patterns appear.
    """

    def test_password_not_in_logs(self, client, registered_user, capsys):
        login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        captured = capsys.readouterr()
        password = registered_user["password"]
        assert password not in captured.out, "Plaintext password must not appear in logs"

    def test_session_id_not_in_logs(self, client, registered_user, capsys):
        resp = login(client, registered_user["username"], registered_user["password"])
        session_id = resp.cookies.get(settings.COOKIE_NAME, "")
        captured = capsys.readouterr()
        if session_id:
            assert session_id not in captured.out, (
                "Session ID (bearer credential) must not appear in logs"
            )

    def test_jwt_pattern_not_in_logs(self, capsys):
        """If a JWT were accidentally passed as a password, it must be redacted."""
        fake_jwt = "[SCRUBBED-SECRET-SHAPED-STRING]"
        # The redaction processor should catch this
        import structlog
        log = structlog.get_logger("test")
        log.info("test_event", some_field=fake_jwt)
        captured = capsys.readouterr()
        assert fake_jwt not in captured.out, "JWT pattern must be redacted in logs"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Input validation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestInputValidation:
    def test_empty_username_rejected(self, client):
        resp = client.post("/auth/login", json={"username": "", "password": "pw"})
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_extra_fields_rejected(self, client):
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login",
            json={"username": "u", "password": "pw", "role": "admin"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 422, "Extra fields must be rejected (CWE-915)"

    def test_missing_password_rejected(self, client):
        resp = client.post("/auth/login", json={"username": "user"})
        assert resp.status_code == 422
```

## Step 3: Audit Checklist Verification

Going through every `## Audit checklist` item from the standards:

### Rules/02 — Authentication

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| argon2id with current-policy parameters, rehash-on-login | ✅ `_ph` with m=65536,t=3,p=4; `check_needs_rehash` on success |
| Login/registration/reset rate-limited, uniform errors/timing | ✅ per-IP + per-account sliding window; dummy hash for timing; identical 401 body |
| Session ID regenerated at login, invalidated server-side at logout | ✅ `session_store.create()` on every login; `session_store.delete()` on logout |
| Sessions have idle + absolute timeouts, registry enables "revoke all" | ✅ `SESSION_IDLE_SECONDS`, `SESSION_ABSOLUTE_SECONDS`; `revoke_all_for_user()` |
| JWT algorithm pinning + claim checks | N/A — opaque session tokens used, not JWTs |
| Tokens out of localStorage and URLs | ✅ HttpOnly cookie only; never in response body |
| "Remember me" selector/validator pattern | N/A — not implemented (out of scope; noted) |
| Account recovery MFA-equivalent | N/A — not in scope for this endpoint |
| Zero hand-rolled token schemes | ✅ CSPRNG via `secrets.token_urlsafe`; argon2-cffi library |

### Rules/04 — Cryptography

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| CSPRNG for all tokens (≥128-bit) | ✅ `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)` = 256-bit |
| No hardcoded secrets | ✅ pepper from env; `_require()` enforces it |
| Constant-time comparison | ✅ argon2-cffi `verify()` is constant-time; `hmac.new` for pepper |
| Stored tokens hashed at rest | ✅ passwords as argon2id hashes; session IDs in memory store (replace with Redis — IDs are opaque, not sensitive in isolation but should be treated as bearer tokens) |
| TLS enforced | ✅ HSTS header set; note in code that TLS termination is at proxy |
| No MD5/SHA-1 in security paths | ✅ SHA-256 used only for username correlation hashes in logs (non-secret use) |

### Rules/05 — Web Platform Security

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| CSP set | ✅ `default-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'none'` |
| CSRF | ✅ SameSite=Lax on cookie; this is a JSON API with Bearer-equivalent (cookie + SameSite) — CSRF token layer should be added for HTML form contexts |
| CORS | Not configured — add an explicit allowlist if a browser frontend calls this directly |
| Security headers baseline | ✅ HSTS, nosniff, Referrer-Policy, COOP, CORP, Permissions-Policy |
| Session cookie `__Host-` prefix, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite | ✅ all set |
| No-store on auth responses | ✅ `_no_cache_response()` on all auth responses |

### Rules/07 — Output & Logging

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| Global boundary handler → generic client message + correlation ID | ✅ `unhandled_exception_handler` |
| No stack traces/paths/SQL in responses | ✅ generic error shape only |
| No existence/secret oracles | ✅ identical 401 for all auth failures; dummy hash for timing |
| Logger-level redaction filter | ✅ `redact_processor` in structlog pipeline |
| CRLF sanitized in logs | ✅ in `redact_processor` |
| Security events logged | ✅ login success/failure, rate-limit, lockout, logout |
| No secrets/PII in query strings | ✅ credentials in POST body only |
| Explicit output DTOs, no raw entity serialization | ✅ `LoginResponse` model |

### Universal Non-Negotiables

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| **(a) Rate limiting** | ✅ per-IP sliding window + per-account sliding window + lockout |
| **(b) TLS/HSTS** | ✅ HSTS header enforced; TLS termination at proxy (stated explicitly) |
| **(c) Tests** | ✅ 9 test classes, 30+ assertions covering all paths |
| **(d) Structured logging without secrets/PII** | ✅ structlog JSON + redaction processor |

---

## Step 4: Verification Status

**I did not execute this test suite** — I have no runtime environment. I will not claim it passes. What I can state:

- The test file is syntactically complete and follows pytest conventions
- Every test assertion targets a specific, observable behavior
- The controls are not silent no-ops: rate limiting returns 429 (tested), the dummy hash adds measurable latency (tested with a > 50ms assertion), session invalidation is verified by querying the store after logout, and the redaction filter is tested by asserting plaintext passwords don't appear in captured stdout

**To verify yourself:**

```bash
pip install -e ".[test]"
AUTH_PEPPER=test-pepper-not-for-prod pytest auth/tests/test_login.py -v
```

The timing test (`TestTimingOracle`) will be slow by design — argon2id with these parameters takes ~0.5s per hash, so 6 samples = ~3s. That is the intended behavior.

```python
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from enum import Enum


class DocumentCategory(str, Enum):
    LEGAL = "legal"
    FINANCIAL = "financial"
    HR = "hr"
    TECHNICAL = "technical"
    MARKETING = "marketing"
    GENERAL = "general"


# ── Database row representation ──────────────────────────────────────────────

class DocumentDB(BaseModel):
    """Represents a document stored in the (simulated) database."""
    id: str
    title: str
    content: str
    category: DocumentCategory
    organization_id: str
    created_by: str
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime
    tags: list[str] = []


class UserDB(BaseModel):
    """Represents a user stored in the (simulated) database."""
    id: str
    username: str
    hashed_password: str
    organization_id: str
    is_active: bool = True


# ── Request / Response schemas ────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TokenResponse(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str = "bearer"


class LoginRequest(BaseModel):
    username: str
    password: str


class DocumentSearchRequest(BaseModel):
    keyword: str = Field(
        ...,
        min_length=1,
        max_length=200,
        description="Keyword to search in title, content, and tags",
    )
    category: Optional[DocumentCategory] = Field(
        None,
        description="Optional category filter",
    )
    page: int = Field(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number (1-based)")
    page_size: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, le=100, description="Results per page")


class DocumentResult(BaseModel):
    id: str
    title: str
    # Return a snippet instead of full content for large docs
    content_snippet: str
    category: DocumentCategory
    created_by: str
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime
    tags: list[str]
    relevance_score: float = Field(description="Simple keyword match score 0-1")


class SearchResponse(BaseModel):
    total_matches: int
    page: int
    page_size: int
    total_pages: int
    keyword: str
    category_filter: Optional[DocumentCategory]
    results: list[DocumentResult]


class CurrentUser(BaseModel):
    id: str
    username: str
    organization_id: str
```

### `auth.py`
```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
from jose import JWTError, jwt

from database import get_user_by_username
from models import CurrentUser

# ── Configuration (use env vars / secrets manager in production) ──────────────
SECRET_KEY = "CHANGE_ME_USE_A_REAL_SECRET_IN_PROD_32CHARS+"
ALGORITHM = "HS256"
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 60

oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="/auth/login")


def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: timedelta | None = None) -> str:
    payload = data.copy()
    expire = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + (
        expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)
    )
    payload["exp"] = expire
    return jwt.encode(payload, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)


def decode_token(token: str) -> dict:
    """Decode and validate a JWT. Raises HTTPException on failure."""
    credentials_exception = HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
        detail="Could not validate credentials",
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
        username: str | None = payload.get("sub")
        if username is None:
            raise credentials_exception
        return payload
    except JWTError:
        raise credentials_exception


def get_current_user(token: Annotated[str, Depends(oauth2_scheme)]) -> CurrentUser:
    """FastAPI dependency — resolves the authenticated user from the JWT."""
    payload = decode_token(token)
    username: str = payload["sub"]

    user = get_user_by_username(username)
    if user is None or not user.is_active:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="User not found or inactive",
        )

    return CurrentUser(
        id=user.id,
        username=user.username,
        organization_id=user.organization_id,
    )
```

### `routes/search.py`
```python
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status

from auth import get_current_user
from database import get_documents_for_org
from models import (
    CurrentUser,
    DocumentCategory,
    DocumentResult,
    DocumentSearchRequest,
    SearchResponse,
)

router = APIRouter(prefix="/documents", tags=["Document Search"])


# ── Helper: relevance scoring ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _compute_relevance(doc, keyword: str) -> float:
    """
    Simple TF-style relevance score.
    Weights: title match (0.5) > tag match (0.3) > content match (0.2).
    Returns 0.0 if the keyword is not found anywhere.
    """
    kw = keyword.lower()
    score = 0.0

    title_words = doc.title.lower().split()
    content_words = doc.content.lower().split()
    tag_words = [t.lower() for t in doc.tags]

    # Title: weighted by frequency, capped at 1.0
    title_hits = sum(1 for w in title_words if kw in w)
    score += min(title_hits / max(len(title_words), 1), 1.0) * 0.5

    # Tags: exact substring match
    tag_hits = sum(1 for t in tag_words if kw in t)
    score += min(tag_hits / max(len(tag_words), 1), 1.0) * 0.3

    # Content: frequency-based
    content_hits = sum(1 for w in content_words if kw in w)
    score += min(content_hits / max(len(content_words), 1), 1.0) * 0.2

    return round(score, 4)


def _make_snippet(content: str, keyword: str, window: int = 150) -> str:
    """Return a short snippet of content around the first keyword occurrence."""
    idx = content.lower().find(keyword.lower())
    if idx == -1:
        return content[:window] + ("..." if len(content) > window else "")

    start = max(0, idx - window // 2)
    end = min(len(content), idx + window // 2)
    snippet = content[start:end]

    if start > 0:
        snippet = "..." + snippet
    if end < len(content):
        snippet = snippet + "..."

    return snippet


# ── Search endpoint ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@router.get("/search", response_model=SearchResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK)
def search_documents(
    current_user: Annotated[CurrentUser, Depends(get_current_user)],
    keyword: str = Query(..., min_length=1, max_length=200, description="Search keyword"),
    category: DocumentCategory | None = Query(None, description="Filter by category"),
    page: int = Query(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number"),
    page_size: int = Query(default=10, ge=1, le=100, description="Results per page"),
) -> SearchResponse:
    """
    Search documents within the authenticated user's organization.

    - Searches **title**, **content**, and **tags** for the keyword.
    - Optionally filters by **category**.
    - Results are ranked by **relevance score** (descending).
    - Supports **pagination** via `page` and `page_size`.
    """
    # 1. Fetch only this user's org documents (enforces data isolation)
    org_docs = get_documents_for_org(current_user.organization_id)

    # 2. Apply category filter
    if category:
        org_docs = [d for d in org_docs if d.category == category]

    # 3. Keyword matching — search title, content, and tags
    kw_lower = keyword.lower()
    matched = []
    for doc in org_docs:
        searchable = f"{doc.title} {doc.content} {' '.join(doc.tags)}".lower()
        if kw_lower in searchable:
            score = _compute_relevance(doc, keyword)
            matched.append((doc, score))

    # 4. Sort by relevance score descending
    matched.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

    # 5. Paginate
    total_matches = len(matched)
    total_pages = max(1, -(-total_matches // page_size))  # ceiling division

    if page > total_pages and total_matches > 0:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail=f"Page {page} exceeds total pages ({total_pages}).",
        )

    start = (page - 1) * page_size
    end = start + page_size
    page_items = matched[start:end]

    # 6. Build response
    results = [
        DocumentResult(
            id=doc.id,
            title=doc.title,
            content_snippet=_make_snippet(doc.content, keyword),
            category=doc.category,
            created_by=doc.created_by,
            created_at=doc.created_at,
            updated_at=doc.updated_at,
            tags=doc.tags,
            relevance_score=score,
        )
        for doc, score in page_items
    ]

    return SearchResponse(
        total_matches=total_matches,
        page=page,
        page_size=page_size,
        total_pages=total_pages,
        keyword=keyword,
        category_filter=category,
        results=results,
    )


# ── POST variant (accepts JSON body) ─────────────────────────────────────────

@router.post("/search", response_model=SearchResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK)
def search_documents_post(
    body: DocumentSearchRequest,
    current_user: Annotated[CurrentUser, Depends(get_current_user)],
) -> SearchResponse:
    """
    Same as GET /documents/search but accepts search parameters in the request body.
    Useful when keyword or filters are complex.
    """
    return search_documents(
        current_user=current_user,
        keyword=body.keyword,
        category=body.category,
        page=body.page,
        page_size=body.page_size,
    )
```

### `main.py`
```python
from datetime import timedelta
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordRequestForm

from auth import ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES, create_access_token, get_current_user
from database import get_user_by_username, verify_password
from models import CurrentUser, LoginRequest, TokenResponse
from routes.search import router as search_router

app = FastAPI(
    title="Document Search API",
    description="Search your organization's documents by keyword and category.",
    version="1.0.0",
)

# ── Auth routes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@app.post("/auth/login", response_model=TokenResponse, tags=["Auth"])
def login(form_data: Annotated[OAuth2PasswordRequestForm, Depends()]) -> TokenResponse:
    """
    Authenticate with username + password.
    Returns a Bearer JWT to use in subsequent requests.

    **Test credentials:**
    - alice / secret123  → org_acme
    - bob   / hunter2    → org_globex
    """
    user = get_user_by_username(form_data.username)
    if not user or not verify_password(form_data.password, user.hashed_password):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Incorrect username or password",
            headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
        )

    token = create_access_token(
        data={"sub": user.username, "org": user.organization_id},
        expires_delta=timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES),
    )
    return TokenResponse(access_token=token)


# ── Protected routes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

app.include_router(search_router)


@app.get("/me", tags=["Auth"])
def read_current_user(
    current_user: Annotated[CurrentUser, Depends(get_current_user)]
) -> CurrentUser:
    """Returns the currently authenticated user's profile."""
    return current_user


# ── Health check ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@app.get("/health", tags=["Ops"])
def health() -> dict:
    return {"status": "ok"}
```


**Step 1 — Get a token:**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/login \
  -d "username=alice&password=secret123" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
```

**Step 2 — Search (GET):**
```bash
curl "http://localhost:8000/documents/search?keyword=policy&category=hr" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>"
```

**Step 3 — Search (POST with body):**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/documents/search \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"keyword": "financial", "category": "financial", "page": 1, "page_size": 5}'
```

**Interactive docs:** `http://localhost:8000/docs`

---

### Key Design Decisions

| Concern | Approach |
|---|---|
| **Auth** | JWT Bearer tokens via `python-jose`; `OAuth2PasswordBearer` dependency |
| **Org isolation** | All queries pre-filter by `organization_id` — users can never see other orgs' data |
| **Search** | Case-insensitive substring match across title, content, tags |
| **Ranking** | Weighted relevance score (title 50%, tags 30%, content 20%) |
| **Snippets** | Context window around first keyword hit instead of full content |
| **Pagination** | Page + page_size with ceiling-division total pages |
| **Both GET & POST** | GET for simple queries; POST for complex/long keyword payloads |

with.artifact:
# Document Search API — Build Plan

## Requirements (concrete, checkable)

1. `GET /api/v1/documents/search` accepts `q` (keyword), `category` (optional enum), `limit`, `cursor` (keyset pagination)
2. Returns only documents belonging to the authenticated user's `tenant_id` (derived from JWT, never from request)
3. Full-text search via PostgreSQL `tsvector` — no raw interpolation
4. Category filter via allowlist mapping — no raw enum passthrough to SQL
5. Keyset pagination with opaque cursor, server-enforced max limit
6. Explicit output DTO — no ORM entity serialization
7. Rate limiting: per-principal sliding window, shared Redis store, `429` + `Retry-After`
8. Structured JSON logging (wide event per request, no secrets/PII)
9. RFC 9457 `application/problem+json` errors, no stack traces to client
10. JWT validation: `iss`, `aud`, `exp`, algorithm allowlist
11. Input validation: allowlist fields, length caps, canonicalization
12. Tests covering: happy path, cross-tenant isolation, auth failure, rate limit, bad input, pagination

## Code

### `migrations/001_documents.sql`

```sql
-- Run once; idempotent via IF NOT EXISTS / CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
    id          UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id   UUID        NOT NULL,
    owner_id    UUID        NOT NULL,
    title       TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(title) <= 500),
    body        TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (char_length(body) <= 1000000),
    category    TEXT        NOT NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    deleted_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,           -- soft-delete; always filtered in app + RLS
    search_vec  TSVECTOR GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
        to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title,'') || ' ' || coalesce(body,''))
    ) STORED
);

-- Tenant isolation enforced at DB level (defense-in-depth behind app-layer check)
ALTER TABLE documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE documents FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON documents
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::uuid);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_search
    ON documents USING GIN (search_vec);

-- Composite index: equality (tenant_id, category) → sort/keyset (created_at, id)
-- Serves: WHERE tenant_id=$1 AND category=$2 AND deleted_at IS NULL
--         ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC with cursor seek
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_tenant_cat_created
    ON documents (tenant_id, category, created_at DESC, id DESC)
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Index without category for non-filtered queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_documents_tenant_created
    ON documents (tenant_id, created_at DESC, id DESC)
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
```

```python
from functools import lru_cache

from pydantic import AnyHttpUrl, field_validator
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):

    search_default_limit: int = 20
    search_max_query_length: int = 256

    # TLS enforcement

            raise ValueError("SQLite is not supported in production")
        return v


@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
```

### `app/logging_config.py`

```python
"""
Structured JSON logging via structlog.
Wide-event accumulator injected per request by correlation middleware.
Redaction filter blocks secrets/PII from reaching any sink.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
from contextvars import ContextVar
from typing import Any

import structlog
from structlog.types import EventDict, WrappedLogger

# ── Redaction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

_SECRET_KEYS = re.compile(
    r"(?i)(password|passwd|secret|token|api[_-]?key|authorization|"
    r"cookie|ssn|card.?number|cvv|private.?key)"
)
_JWT_SHAPE = re.compile(r"eyJ[\w-]{10,}\.[\w-]{10,}\.[\w-]+")
_BEARER = re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[\w\-\.]+")


def _redact_processor(
    _logger: WrappedLogger, _method: str, event_dict: EventDict
) -> EventDict:
    """Walk the event dict and redact secrets.  Runs at every log call."""
    def _scrub(obj: Any, depth: int = 0) -> Any:
        if depth > 8:
            return obj
        if isinstance(obj, dict):
            return {
                k: "[REDACTED]" if _SECRET_KEYS.search(str(k)) else _scrub(v, depth + 1)
                for k, v in obj.items()
            }
        if isinstance(obj, str):
            obj = _JWT_SHAPE.sub("[JWT]", obj)
            obj = _BEARER.sub("Bearer [REDACTED]", obj)
            # Strip CRLF to prevent log injection (CWE-117)
            obj = obj.replace("\r", "\\r").replace("\n", "\\n")
            return obj
        if isinstance(obj, (list, tuple)):
            return type(obj)(_scrub(i, depth + 1) for i in obj)
        return obj

    return {k: _scrub(v) for k, v in event_dict.items()}


# ── Wide-event accumulator ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

_wide_event: ContextVar[dict[str, Any]] = ContextVar("_wide_event", default={})


def get_wide_event() -> dict[str, Any]:
    return _wide_event.get()


def set_wide_event_field(key: str, value: Any) -> None:
    _wide_event.get()[key] = value


def reset_wide_event(initial: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    _wide_event.set(initial)


# ── structlog configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

def configure_logging(service_name: str, version: str, environment: str) -> None:
    shared_processors: list[Any] = [
        structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,
        structlog.stdlib.add_log_level,
        structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name,
        structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso", utc=True),
        _redact_processor,
    ]

    if environment == "development":
        renderer: Any = structlog.dev.ConsoleRenderer()
    else:
        renderer = structlog.processors.JSONRenderer()

    structlog.configure(
        processors=shared_processors
        + [
            structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.wrap_for_formatter,
        ],
        wrapper_class=structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger,
        context_class=dict,
        logger_factory=structlog.stdlib.LoggerFactory(),
        cache_logger_on_first_use=True,
    )

    formatter = structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter(
        processor=renderer,
        foreign_pre_chain=shared_processors,
    )

    handler = logging.StreamHandler()
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)

    root = logging.getLogger()
    root.handlers = [handler]
    root.setLevel(logging.INFO)

    # Bind stable service dimensions once; per-request fields added by middleware
    structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(
        service=service_name,
        version=version,
        environment=environment,
    )
```

### `app/middleware/correlation.py`

```python
"""
1. Generates / propagates W3C trace context.
2. Creates the per-request wide-event accumulator.
3. Emits the wide event in a finally block (fires on exceptions too).
4. Enforces HTTPS in production.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
import uuid
from typing import Callable

import structlog
from fastapi import Request, Response
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse
from starlette.types import ASGIApp

from app.config import get_settings
from app.logging_config import reset_wide_event, get_wide_event, set_wide_event_field

logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


class CorrelationAndLoggingMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp) -> None:
        super().__init__(app)
        self._settings = get_settings()

    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next: Callable) -> Response:
        settings = self._settings

        # ── HTTPS enforcement ────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if settings.enforce_https and request.url.scheme == "http":
            # Allow loopback health checks; block everything else
            host = request.client.host if request.client else ""
            if not host.startswith("127.") and host != "::1":
                return JSONResponse(
                    status_code=301,
                    headers={"Location": str(request.url).replace("http://", "https://", 1)},
                )

        # ── Correlation ID ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
        # Accept W3C traceparent from upstream; generate otherwise
        traceparent = request.headers.get("traceparent")
        if traceparent:
            # Extract trace-id segment (positions 3-34 in "00-<trace_id>-<span_id>-<flags>")
            parts = traceparent.split("-")
            trace_id = parts[1] if len(parts) >= 4 else str(uuid.uuid4().hex)
        else:
            trace_id = uuid.uuid4().hex

        request_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

        # Bind to structlog context for this request (contextvars — async-safe)
        structlog.contextvars.clear_contextvars()
        structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(
            trace_id=trace_id,
            request_id=request_id,
            service=settings.service_name,
            version=settings.service_version,
            environment=settings.environment,
        )

        # ── Wide-event accumulator ───────────────────────────────────────────
        reset_wide_event({
            "event": "http_request",
            "trace_id": trace_id,
            "request_id": request_id,
            "http.request.method": request.method,
            "http.route": request.url.path,
        })

        start = time.perf_counter()
        response: Response | None = None
        try:
            response = await call_next(request)
            set_wide_event_field("http.response.status_code", response.status_code)
            set_wide_event_field(
                "outcome", "success" if response.status_code < 400 else "error"
            )
            return response
        except Exception as exc:
            set_wide_event_field("http.response.status_code", 500)
            set_wide_event_field("outcome", "error")
            set_wide_event_field("error.type", type(exc).__name__)
            raise
        finally:
            duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
            set_wide_event_field("duration_ms", duration_ms)

            wide = get_wide_event()
            # Emit at WARN if slow (>2s), INFO otherwise
            if duration_ms > 2000:
                logger.warning("http_request_slow", **wide)
            else:
                logger.info("http_request", **wide)

            # Propagate trace ID to client for support correlation
            if response is not None:
                response.headers["X-Request-Id"] = request_id
                response.headers["X-Trace-Id"] = trace_id
                # HSTS — only meaningful over TLS; harmless otherwise
                response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = (
                    "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload"
                )
```

### `app/middleware/error_handler.py`

```python
"""
Global exception → RFC 9457 problem+json converter.
No stack traces, SQL, or internal paths reach the client.
Full detail goes to the server-side log keyed by trace_id.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import structlog
from fastapi import Request
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException

logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


def _problem(
    status: int,
    type_slug: str,
    title: str,
    detail: str,
    request: Request,
    extra: dict | None = None,
) -> JSONResponse:
    trace_id = request.headers.get("X-Trace-Id", "")
    body: dict = {
        "type": f"https://api.example.com/errors/{type_slug}",
        "title": title,
        "status": status,
        "detail": detail,
        "instance": str(request.url.path),
        "trace_id": trace_id,
    }
    if extra:
        body.update(extra)
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=status,
        content=body,
        media_type="application/problem+json",
    )


async def http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException) -> JSONResponse:
    # 5xx: log full detail server-side, return generic body
    if exc.status_code >= 500:
        logger.error(
            "http_error",
            status=exc.status_code,
            detail=str(exc.detail),
        )
        return _problem(exc.status_code, "internal-error", "Internal error",
                        "An unexpected error occurred.", request)
    return _problem(
        exc.status_code,
        {
            400: "bad-request", 401: "unauthorized", 403: "forbidden",
            404: "not-found", 405: "method-not-allowed", 409: "conflict",
            422: "validation-error", 429: "rate-limited",
        }.get(exc.status_code, "error"),
        exc.detail if isinstance(exc.detail, str) else "Error",
        exc.detail if isinstance(exc.detail, str) else str(exc.detail),
        request,
    )


async def validation_exception_handler(
    request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError
) -> JSONResponse:
    errors = [
        {
            "pointer": "/" + "/".join(str(loc) for loc in e["loc"]),
            "code": e["type"],
            "message": e["msg"],
        }
        for e in exc.errors()
    ]
    return _problem(
        422, "validation-error", "Validation failed",
        f"{len(errors)} field(s) failed validation.",
        request,
        {"errors": errors},
    )


async def unhandled_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception) -> JSONResponse:
    # Log full exception server-side; client gets only correlation ID
    logger.exception("unhandled_exception", exc_info=exc)
    return _problem(500, "internal-error", "Internal error",
                    "An unexpected error occurred.", request)
```

### `app/auth.py`

```python
"""
JWT validation: RS256/ES256 only, iss+aud+exp enforced, JWKS with rotation.
Returns a validated principal; never trusts tenant_id from the request.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
from functools import lru_cache
from typing import Annotated
from uuid import UUID

import httpx
import structlog
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, Security
from fastapi.security import HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, HTTPBearer
from jose import ExpiredSignatureError, JWTError, jwk, jwt
from jose.exceptions import JWKError
from pydantic import BaseModel, field_validator

from app.config import Settings, get_settings

logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

_bearer = HTTPBearer(auto_error=True)

# ── JWKS cache (rotated on 401 from the validator) ───────────────────────────

_jwks_cache: dict = {}
_jwks_fetched_at: float = 0.0
_JWKS_TTL = 3600.0  # re-fetch after 1 h


async def _fetch_jwks(settings: Settings) -> dict:
    global _jwks_cache, _jwks_fetched_at
    now = time.monotonic()
    if _jwks_cache and (now - _jwks_fetched_at) < _JWKS_TTL:
        return _jwks_cache
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:
        resp = await client.get(str(settings.jwt_jwks_uri))
        resp.raise_for_status()
    _jwks_cache = resp.json()
    _jwks_fetched_at = now
    return _jwks_cache


# ── Principal model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class Principal(BaseModel):
    """Validated claims from the JWT.  tenant_id is authoritative here."""
    sub: str            # opaque user identifier
    tenant_id: UUID     # from token claim — never from request body/params
    email: str | None = None   # optional; present only if IDP includes it
    scopes: list[str] = []

    @field_validator("tenant_id", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def coerce_uuid(cls, v: object) -> UUID:
        try:
            return UUID(str(v))
        except (ValueError, AttributeError) as exc:
            raise ValueError("invalid tenant_id in token") from exc


# ── Dependency ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def get_current_principal(
    credentials: Annotated[HTTPAuthorizationCredentials, Security(_bearer)],
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> Principal:
    token = credentials.credentials
    exc_401 = HTTPException(
        status_code=401,
        detail="invalid_credentials",   # uniform message — no oracle (CWE-203)
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )

    # ── Algorithm allowlist — never 'none', never symmetric ──────────────────
    allowed_algorithms = [settings.jwt_algorithm]   # RS256 or ES256 only

    try:
        # Peek at header to get kid for JWKS lookup
        unverified_header = jwt.get_unverified_header(token)
    except JWTError:
        logger.warning("jwt_invalid_header")
        raise exc_401

    if unverified_header.get("alg") not in allowed_algorithms:
        logger.warning("jwt_algorithm_rejected", alg=unverified_header.get("alg"))
        raise exc_401

    try:
        jwks = await _fetch_jwks(settings)
        # Find the matching key by kid
        kid = unverified_header.get("kid")
        key_data = next(
            (k for k in jwks.get("keys", []) if k.get("kid") == kid), None
        )
        if key_data is None:
            # Rotate cache once and retry
            _jwks_fetched_at = 0.0
            jwks = await _fetch_jwks(settings)
            key_data = next(
                (k for k in jwks.get("keys", []) if k.get("kid") == kid), None
            )
        if key_data is None:
            logger.warning("jwt_kid_not_found", kid=kid)
            raise exc_401

        public_key = jwk.construct(key_data)
        payload = jwt.decode(
            token,
            public_key,
            algorithms=allowed_algorithms,
            audience=settings.jwt_audience,
            issuer=settings.jwt_issuer,
            options={
                "verify_exp": True,
                "verify_aud": True,
                "verify_iss": True,
                "require_exp": True,
                "require_iat": True,
            },
        )
    except ExpiredSignatureError:
        logger.info("jwt_expired")   # INFO — expected; not an error
        raise exc_401
    except (JWTError, JWKError, httpx.HTTPError) as exc:
        logger.warning("jwt_validation_failed", error=type(exc).__name__)
        raise exc_401

    try:
        principal = Principal(
            sub=payload["sub"],
            tenant_id=payload.get("tenant_id") or payload.get("tid") or "",
            email=payload.get("email"),
            scopes=payload.get("scope", "").split() if payload.get("scope") else [],
        )
    except (KeyError, ValueError) as exc:
        logger.warning("jwt_claims_invalid", error=str(exc))
        raise exc_401

    # Bind to log context for the rest of this request
    structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(
        user_id=principal.sub,
        tenant_id=str(principal.tenant_id),
    )
    return principal
```

```python
"""
Async SQLAlchemy engine + session factory.
Sets app.tenant_id on every connection via SET LOCAL so RLS policies fire.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from typing import AsyncGenerator
from uuid import UUID

from sqlalchemy import event, text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import (
    AsyncConnection,
    AsyncSession,
    async_sessionmaker,
    create_async_engine,
)

from app.config import get_settings

settings = get_settings()

engine = create_async_engine(
    settings.database_url,
    pool_size=10,
    max_overflow=20,
    pool_pre_ping=True,  # evict stale connections
    echo=False,          # never True in production (logs SQL with params)
)

AsyncSessionLocal = async_sessionmaker(
    engine,
    class_=AsyncSession,
    expire_on_commit=False,
    autocommit=False,
    autoflush=False,
)


@asynccontextmanager
async def get_db_session(
    tenant_id: UUID,
) -> AsyncGenerator[AsyncSession, None]:
    """
    Yields a session with RLS tenant context set for its lifetime.
    tenant_id comes from the validated JWT principal — never from request params.
    """
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
        async with session.begin():
            # SET LOCAL scopes the variable to this transaction — safe for pooling
            await session.execute(
                text("SET LOCAL app.tenant_id = :tid"),
                {"tid": str(tenant_id)},
            )
            yield session
            # Transaction commits on clean exit; rolls back on exception
```

### `app/rate_limit.py`

```python
"""
Sliding-window rate limiter backed by Redis (atomic Lua script).
Keyed per authenticated principal — not per IP for authenticated routes.
Returns (allowed, limit, remaining, reset_in_seconds).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time

import structlog
from redis.asyncio import Redis

logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

# Atomic sliding-window via Lua — no TOCTOU between ZREMRANGEBYSCORE and ZADD
_SLIDING_WINDOW_SCRIPT = """
local key      = KEYS[1]
local now      = tonumber(ARGV[1])
local window   = tonumber(ARGV[2])
local limit    = tonumber(ARGV[3])
local jitter   = tonumber(ARGV[4])

local cutoff = now - window * 1000   -- milliseconds

-- Remove entries outside the window
redis.call('ZREMRANGEBYSCORE', key, '-inf', cutoff)

local count = redis.call('ZCARD', key)
if count >= limit then
    local oldest = redis.call('ZRANGE', key, 0, 0, 'WITHSCORES')
    local reset_ms = tonumber(oldest[2]) + window * 1000
    return {0, count, 0, math.ceil((reset_ms - now) / 1000)}
end

-- Add this request with a unique member (now + jitter avoids collisions at ms granularity)
redis.call('ZADD', key, now, now .. ':' .. jitter)
redis.call('PEXPIRE', key, window * 1000)
local remaining = limit - count - 1
return {1, limit, remaining, window}
"""


async def check_rate_limit(
    redis: Redis,
    principal_id: str,
    endpoint: str,
    limit: int,
    window_seconds: int,
) -> tuple[bool, int, int, int]:
    """
    Returns (allowed, limit, remaining, retry_after_seconds).
    Raises no exceptions — on Redis failure, ALLOW (fail open is preferred
    over denying all traffic; deploy a circuit breaker at the gateway for
    hard guarantees).
    """
    key = f"rl:{endpoint}:{principal_id}"
    now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
    jitter = id(object())  # cheap unique-enough suffix within a ms

    try:
        result = await redis.eval(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            _SLIDING_WINDOW_SCRIPT,
            1,
            key,
            now_ms,
            window_seconds,
            limit,
            jitter,
        )
        allowed, lim, remaining, retry_after = (int(x) for x in result)
        if not allowed:
            logger.warning(
                "rate_limit_exceeded",
                principal_id=principal_id,
                endpoint=endpoint,
                retry_after=retry_after,
            )
        return bool(allowed), lim, remaining, retry_after
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        # Redis unavailable — log and fail open (gateway is the hard backstop)
        logger.error(
            "rate_limit_redis_error",
            error=type(exc).__name__,
            principal_id=principal_id,
        )
        return True, limit, limit, 0
```

### `app/schemas.py`

```python
"""
Explicit input and output DTOs.
Input: allowlist fields, length caps, canonicalized, extra='forbid'.
Output: explicit allowlist — no ORM entity fields leak through.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import base64
import json
import unicodedata
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Annotated
from uuid import UUID

from pydantic import (
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    field_validator,
    model_validator,
)

from app.config import get_settings

_settings = get_settings()


# ── Allowlisted categories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class DocumentCategory(str, Enum):
    """
    Hardcoded allowlist.  Adding a category requires a code change.
    Never interpolated directly into SQL — mapped to a string literal.
    """
    CONTRACTS   = "contracts"
    INVOICES    = "invoices"
    REPORTS     = "reports"
    POLICIES    = "policies"
    OTHER       = "other"


# Map enum → exact DB string.  Indirection means a typo in the enum
# doesn't silently pass a wrong value to the query.
CATEGORY_DB_MAP: dict[DocumentCategory, str] = {
    DocumentCategory.CONTRACTS: "contracts",
    DocumentCategory.INVOICES:  "invoices",
    DocumentCategory.REPORTS:   "reports",
    DocumentCategory.POLICIES:  "policies",
    DocumentCategory.OTHER:     "other",
}

# Allowlisted sort fields → exact SQL column expressions
SORT_FIELD_MAP: dict[str, str] = {
    "created_at": "created_at",
    "title":      "title",
}


# ── Cursor (opaque, base64-encoded JSON) ──────────────────────────────────────

class SearchCursor(BaseModel):
    created_at: datetime
    id: UUID


def encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, doc_id: UUID) -> str:
    payload = {"created_at": created_at.isoformat(), "id": str(doc_id)}
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps(payload).encode()).decode()


def decode_cursor(raw: str) -> SearchCursor:
    try:
        data = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(raw.encode() + b"=="))
        return SearchCursor(**data)
    except Exception as exc:
        raise ValueError("invalid cursor") from exc


# ── Input DTO ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class DocumentSearchRequest(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")   # unknown query params → 422

    q: Annotated[
        str | None,
        Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=_settings.search_max_query_length),
    ] = None

    category: DocumentCategory | None = None

    limit: Annotated[int, Field(ge=1, le=_settings.search_max_limit)] = (
        _settings.search_default_limit
    )

    cursor: str | None = None

    @field_validator("q", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def canonicalize_query(cls, v: object) -> str | None:
        """NFC-normalize and strip control characters; validate after."""
        if v is None:
            return None
        if not isinstance(v, str):
            raise ValueError("q must be a string")
        # Canonicalize: NFC unicode normalization
        v = unicodedata.normalize("NFC", v)
        # Strip control characters (including CRLF — CWE-117 / injection)
        v = "".join(ch for ch in v if unicodedata.category(ch)[0] != "C")
        v = v.strip()
        if not v:
            return None
        return v

    @field_validator("cursor", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def validate_cursor_shape(cls, v: object) -> str | None:
        if v is None:
            return None
        if not isinstance(v, str):
            raise ValueError("cursor must be a string")
        # Length cap — prevent DoS via huge cursor values
        if len(v) > 512:
            raise ValueError("cursor too long")
        # Validate decodability at input time; fail fast
        try:
            decode_cursor(v)
        except ValueError as exc:
            raise ValueError("invalid cursor") from exc
        return v

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def require_q_or_category(self) -> "DocumentSearchRequest":
        """Require at least one search parameter — prevent open scans."""
        if self.q is None and self.category is None:
            raise ValueError("at least one of 'q' or 'category' must be provided")
        return self


# ── Output DTOs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class DocumentResult(BaseModel):
    """
    Explicit output allowlist.  Adding a DB column changes nothing here.
    tenant_id and owner_id are intentionally omitted from the response.
    """
    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: UUID
    title: str
    category: str
    created_at: datetime
    # snippet: highlighted excerpt from body (populated by the query layer)
    snippet: str | None = None


class DocumentSearchResponse(BaseModel):
    data: list[DocumentResult]
    next_cursor: str | None   # null when no more pages
    has_more: bool
    # total deliberately omitted — COUNT(*) on large tables is a self-DoS
    # Use ?include_total=true as a future opt-in (not implemented here)
```

```python
"""
SQLAlchemy ORM model — used only for type safety; queries use text() with params.
Never serialized directly to responses.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from datetime import datetime

from sqlalchemy import DateTime, Index, String, Text
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import TSVECTOR, UUID as PG_UUID
from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column


class Base(DeclarativeBase):
    pass


class Document(Base):
    __tablename__ = "documents"

    id:         Mapped[uuid.UUID]  = mapped_column(PG_UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    tenant_id:  Mapped[uuid.UUID]  = mapped_column(PG_UUID(as_uuid=True), nullable=False, index=True)
    owner_id:   Mapped[uuid.UUID]  = mapped_column(PG_UUID(as_uuid=True), nullable=False)
    title:      Mapped[str]        = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=False)
    body:       Mapped[str]        = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False)
    category:   Mapped[str]        = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=False)
    created_at: Mapped[datetime]   = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
    updated_at: Mapped[datetime]   = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
    search_vec: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(TSVECTOR, nullable=True)

    __table_args__ = (

              "tenant_id", "category", "created_at", "id",

    )
```
